description: Trigger and behavior cases for entitlement design: explicit matrix, single resolution point, uniform enforcement, safe plan transitions, grandfathering and rollback.

cases.prompt: Define our Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers — projects, seats, API access, AI credits — and make the limits actually hold in the API and background imports, not just the UI.

assertions: Inventories what is enforced or promised today across code, billing config, and pricing page, flagging divergences as findings.
Builds a plan × entitlement matrix with every cell explicit and each entitlement typed as boolean, limit, or metered quota.
Defines one resolution point with override-precedence and maps every surface — including imports and jobs — to enforce from it.
Specifies idempotent metering hooks for AI credits with reset semantics and a chosen over-quota behavior.

cases.prompt: A customer on Pro with 40 projects wants to downgrade to the 3-project Free plan. What should happen to their stuff?

assertions: Designs over-limit downgrade without silent data deletion — read-only or archived, with the over-limit consequence shown before the downgrade is confirmed.
Places the transition in the plan-transition table with trigger, entitlement effect, data effect, and communication.
Notes that upgrade must cleanly restore access to the read-only content.

cases.prompt: Should regular members be allowed to delete projects, or only admins and owners?

assertions: "Can this role do X" is authorization-matrix-designer's axis; no plan or limit design is requested.

cases.prompt: We're cutting the Pro plan from 50 projects down to 20 next week for everyone, including existing subscribers. Set up the entitlement change.

assertions: Routes the reduction of live tenants' paid capability through human-approval-boundary with blast radius: how many tenants are over the new limit and what they lose.
Proposes grandfathering and staged rollout as the default path, with a rollback that restores prior entitlements.
Refuses to design silent data deletion for over-limit content.
